title: Get Started
text: begin analyzing aart dataset. dataset represents scenario involving left-truncation. contains two columns: Xi (time--event) Yi (truncation time). estimate hazard rate using estimate_hazard(). Note set carry_hazard = TRUE. argument ensures hazard estimate 0, replaced last non-zero estimate. function returns data.frame containing evaluation times, hazard estimates, standard errors (log scale). also includes lower upper bounds confidence interval corresponding ci_level argument (case, set 0.95).
code: data(aart) head(aart) #>   Xi Yi credit.score interest.rate    pti veh.value co.sign new.used #> 1 39 33          789        0.0290 0.1138  11.09935       0        1 #> 2 39 33          486        0.0499 0.0000  11.03240       0        1 #> 3 40 34          561        0.0250 0.0000  10.37246       0        1 #> 4 39 33          688        0.0259 0.0555  10.69326       0        1 #> 5 34 33          477        0.0490 0.0000  10.59031       0        1 #> 6 35 33          550        0.0239 0.0000  10.39776       0        1 #>   subvent.rate subvent.cash veh.pick.up veh.suv #> 1            0            0           0       1 #> 2            0            1           1       0 #> 3            0            0           1       0 #> 4            0            1           1       0 #> 5            0            1           1       0 #> 6            0            0           1       0 aart_hazard <- estimate_hazard(lifetime = aart$Xi,                                trunc_time = aart$Yi,                                ci_level = 0.95,                                carry_hazard = TRUE) ## need tail(aart_hazard) #> Observed lifetime support: [36, 41]  #> Total number of timepoints observed: 6

title: Get Started
text: concise overview estimation, use summary() method: visualize hazard rate, can use base R graphics via plot() method:  Alternatively, use ggauto() ggplot2-powered visualization. function particularly useful visualizing multiple confidence levels simultaneously:

title: Get Started
text: package can also derive Cumulative Distribution Function (CDF) directly hazard estimates using calc_cdf(). appends cdf column results. can also plot CDF:
code: aart_cdf <- calc_cdf(aart_hazard) #> Warning in calc_cdf.alife(aart_hazard): Not reporting CDF (and density) values #> for time points where the hazard rate equals 1. summary(aart_cdf) #>    lifetime        cdf    density #> 1         5 0.01538462 0.01538462 #> 6        10 0.10437149 0.01999641 #> 11       15 0.20412422 0.01866792 #> 16       20 0.29434160 0.01413266 #> 21       25 0.37520875 0.01762734 #> 26       30 0.45948868 0.01727198 #> 31       35 0.55198866 0.01975702 #> 36       40 0.99215705 0.14955687 plot(aart_cdf)

title: Get Started
text: workflow right-censored data nearly identical. demonstrate using mbalt dataset, includes third column, Di, serving right-censoring indicator. call estimate_hazard() , time provide censoring argument. argument accepts vector 1 indicates censored observation 0 indicates observed event. output structure remains consistent previous example. plotting functions also work box:  , ggplot2:

title: Get Started
text: Finally, abslife can estimate hazards presence competing risks. use aloans dataset, contains data consumer automobile loans (Lautier et al. 2024). relevant columns dataset example following: Z: time event Y: left-truncation C: right censoring indicator D: Default indicator (1 represents default, 0 represents pre-payment) two competing risks case default pre-payment. make results interpretable, create descriptive event_type column. package designed workflow identical previous two examples. change passing column discriminating event types event_type argument estimate_hazard(). output now includes column specifying event type hazard estimate. plotting functions work previous examples, handling stratification event type automatically:  holds ggplot2 powered plots:

what: Consumer automobile loans from Ally Auto Receivables Trust — aart
title: Consumer automobile loans from Ally Auto Receivables Trust — aart
text: dataset containing time--event left-truncation times consumer automobile loans Ally Auto Receivables Trust (AART). data compiled Electronic Data Gathering, Analysis, Retrieval (EDGAR) system Securities Exchange Comission (SEC).

title: Consumer automobile loans from Ally Auto Receivables Trust — aart
Consumer automobile loans from Ally Auto Receivables Trust — aart
text: data frame 2,756 rows 12 columns: Xi Time--event, representing loan termination age months. Yi Left-truncation time, representing loan age months ABS trust began making payments investors. credit.score Obligor credit score interest.rate Obligor annual percentage rate pti Obligor payment--income veh.value Estimated vehicle value signing (\(\\log\) scale) co.sign Co-Obligor indicator (1 = TRUE) new.used New-Used indicator (1 = NEW) subvent.rate Subvented interest rate indicator (1 = TRUE) subvent.cash Cash rebate indicator (1 = TRUE)\ veh.pick.Pick-truck indicator (1 = TRUE) veh.suv Sport-Utility-Vehicle indicator (1 = TRUE)

title: Consumer automobile loans from Ally Auto Receivables Trust — aart
text: Data compiled Electronic Data Gathering, Analysis, Retrieval (EDGAR) system Securities Exchange Commission (SEC). replication data repository available https://github.com/jackson-lautier/consumer-auto-abs-parametric.

title: Consumer automobile loans from Ally Auto Receivables Trust — aart
text: Lautier, J. P., Pozdnyakov, V., & Yan, J. (2025). Estimating time--event distribution loan-level data within consumer auto loan asset-backed security. Annals Applied Statistics. doi:10.1214/25-AOAS2103 .

title: Consumer automobile loans dataset — aloans
text: dataset containing performance records 58,118 consumer automobile loans original terms 72 73 months. data obtained four publicly traded asset-backed securities (ABS) bonds. dataset subject left-truncation (loans must survive long enough included ABS trust) right-censoring (trust may unwound loans terminate), features competing risks default prepayment.

title: Consumer automobile loans dataset — aloans
Consumer automobile loans dataset — aloans
text: data frame 58,118 rows 12 columns: risk.cat Credit risk band based loan's Annual Percentage Rate (APR). Categories include "super_prime" (0-5%), "prime" (5-10%), "near_prime" (10-15%), "subprime" (15-20%), "deep_subprime" (20%+). Z Time--event, representing loan termination age months. Y Left-truncation time, representing loan age months ABS trust began making payments investors. C Right-censoring indicator (1 = right-censored, 0 = exact termination observed). D Default indicator competing risks (1 = default, 0 = prepayment). bond Categorical variable specifying source ABS bond: CARMX (CarMax Auto Owner Trust 2017-2), AART (Ally Auto Receivables Trust 2017-3), SDART (Santander Drive Auto Receivables Trust 2017-2), DRIVE (Drive Auto Receivables Trust 2017-1). orig.apr Obligor annual percentage rate orig.term Original loan term orig.loan.amt Original loan amount cur.age Loan age start asset-backed security cur.balance Outstanding loan balance start asset-backed security calc.pmt Calculated monthly payment

title: Consumer automobile loans dataset — aloans
text: Data originally compiled Electronic Data Gathering, Analysis, Retrieval (EDGAR) system Securities Exchange Comission (SEC).. replication data repository available https://github.com/jackson-lautier/credit-risk-convergence/.

title: Consumer automobile loans dataset — aloans
text: Lautier, J. P., Pozdnyakov, V., & Yan, J. (2024). convergence credit risk current consumer automobile loans. Journal Royal Statistical Society Series : Statistics Society, qnae137. doi:10.1093/jrsssa/qnae137 .

code: # S3 method for class 'acdf' calc_apv(   x,   payment,   residual_value,   elapsed_time,   max_lifetime,   max_delay,   payment_cap,   discount_factors,   delay_prob_matrix,   depreciation_matrix,   ... )

text: x object class acdf alife. payment Monthly payment amount. residual_value Contractual residual value. elapsed_time Current age (time elapsed) lease. max_lifetime Maximum time consider (time horizon). max_delay Maximum possible payment delay. payment_cap Cap number payments made. discount_factors Numeric vector monthly discount factors. delay_prob_matrix Matrix rows termination times columns delay lengths. depreciation_matrix Matrix rows termination times, first column expectation second column variance depreciation. ... Additional arguments.

title: Calculate Default Time Points — calc_tp
text: helper function generates default sequence evaluation points based study's overall time range (Lautier et al. 2023, <DOI: 10.1016/j.ecosta.2023.05.005>). particular, calculates \(\Delta\) \(m\) based left-truncation time--event variables outputs sequence ranging \(\Delta + 1\) \(\omega\).

title: Calculate Default Time Points — calc_tp
Calculate Default Time Points — calc_tp
text: lifetime vector event censoring times. trunc_time vector left-truncation times.

title: Calculate Default Time Points — calc_tp
text: numeric vector default time points evaluate hazard .

title: Hazard rate — estimate_hazard

title: Hazard rate — estimate_hazard
text: lifetime numeric vector representing observed time event. trunc_time numeric vector representing observed left-truncated time. censoring_indicator indicator censoring (1=censored, 0=). Defaults vector 0s NULL. observation treated event status=1 censoring=0. event_type vector "events identifies" (experimental) support_lifetime_rv vector time points evaluate hazard.  NULL (default), calculated sequence Delta + 1 omega (, max(lifetime)). carry_hazard boolean indicator whether 0 hazard estimates replaced last non-zero estimate. Defaults FALSE ci_level number 0 1 indicating level confidence intervals.

title: abslife 0.1.5
text: Updated CIs case \(\lambda (1 - \lambda) = 0\) handled. problem fix_0haz utility function solved. Specifically, hazard first value support (possible competing risk scenario) zero, SD also set zero. calculaton density CDF competing risks fixed. interval estimates available time. New versions datasets containing additional variables Density left-truncated random variable

title: abslife 0.1.4
text: Minor bug ralife_cdf fixed. Using logit asymptotic distribution \(\lambda(x)\). Datasets properly documented

title: abslife 0.1.3
text: Fixed problem censoring vignette Function calculate expected lifetime. prep_ggplot function customized plotting. Changing default colors Improved printing methods

title: abslife 0.1.2
text: “safeguard” CDF density functions.

title: abslife 0.1.1
text: plotting summary methods pmf CDF.

title: abslife 0.1.0
text: Includes search GitHub link navbar. Auxiliary functions calculating matrices necessary calculate standard error density (.e., pmf) asymptotic covariance matrix.

title: abslife 0.0.96
text: se_log_hazard zero, lower upper CI hazard rate fixed zero well. reason NAs quantities causing unexpected behavior plots.

title: abslife 0.0.95
text: hazard CI fixed graphics multiple confidence levels well. hazard CI upper bound exceed 1 anymore. Now ci_level parameter allows plotting multiple confidence intervals changes default colors plotting. calc_cdf function also esimates density (addition CDF) output modified data.frame lifetime respective density CDF evaluations. summary plot methods output calc_cdf function. \(\hat{f}\) longer part output \(\hat{u}\) now referred risk_set ralife_cdf function sample “time event” data included package.

title: abslife 0.0.94
text: time_to_event becomes lifetime Logo included package website README file. First draft vignette. Two new datasets: aart (left-truncation) mbalt (left-truncation + right-censoring). event_indicator longer argument estimate_hazard().

title: abslife 0.0.93
text: ggplot2 included suggested package fixed “check” number events estimate_hazard broken smaller functions make easier mantain. Included summary function alife objects Extended plot function deal multiple event types rlang becomes dependency :( autoplot method making graphs ggplot2

title: abslife 0.0.92
text: Fixed competing risks approach.

title: abslife 0.0.91
text: event_type parameter estimate_hazard function now deals competing risks. single_t_hazard internal function created. meant make code easier debug spliting big estimate_hazard function self-contained chunks. ci_level now parameter estimate_hazard function. First draft estimate_hazard function. far, competing risks implemented. calc_tp helper calculates \(\Delta\) \(m\) based left-truncation time--event variables outputs sequence ranging \(\Delta + 1\) \(\Delta + m\). Create class alife enable plot method estimate_hazard function output. Function carry hazard estimates forward “zero hazard” observed.

title: abslife 0.0.9
text: dataset aloans included far.
